body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology / Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Ecological self — neuroscience of nature connectedness, biophilia as evolved interoceptive resonance.
**Phase context:** Nature connectedness, biophilia, environmental neuroscience

Paper: 'Becoming human again': Exploring connections between nature and recovery from stress and post-traumatic distress.
DOI: 10.3233/WOR-141934
Abstract: BACKGROUND: Many military veterans are seeking ways beyond conventional treatments to manage their stress injuries. An increasing number is turning to nature, including hiking and fishing, farming and gardening, and building relationships with dogs or horses. Many continue to benefit from medication and therapy, but find that nature provides an additional measure of support, relief and healing in their lives. Many military veterans are seeking ways beyond conventional treatments to manage their stress injuries. An increasing number is turning to nature, including hiking and fishing, farming and gardening, and building relationships with dogs or horses. Many continue to benefit from medication and therapy, but find that nature provides an additional measure of support, relief and healing in their lives. OBJECTIVE: This paper examines reciprocal interactions between humans and nature during post-conflict recovery, with a focus on the experiences of four North American veterans who regard their personal recovery from stressful and traumatic military experiences as intimately tied to their nature experiences.
